evals.prompt: Replay run CFT-20260716-135002-EA345997 with the malformed COWORK_CODEX_AUDIT_EVIDENCE_V1 JSON block and verify that no pull request can be created.
evals.expected_output: The host blocks the create_pull_request call before execution with a dedicated audit-evidence parse code and a concrete validation path.
expectations: The malformed block is rejected before PR creation
The result uses AUDIT_EVIDENCE_BLOCK_PARSE_FAILED
validation_error is audit_artifact.evidence_block:json_parse_failed

evals.prompt: Validate audit snippets containing quotes, backslashes, tabs, Unicode, LF, CR, CRLF, U+2028, U+2029, and a final newline against the cited same-SHA range.
evals.expected_output: Exact content passes and every one-byte or code-unit deviation fails before the PR.
expectations: The exact hostile snippet round-trips through JSON
The exact range content passes
Every tested deviation is rejected

evals.prompt: Replay Bug 17 where a persisted branch plus audit-artifact-without-PR residue is followed by a fresh observation of template PR #5.
evals.expected_output: The PR supersedes the obsolete no-PR residue and the public status contains exactly branch plus PR.
expectations: The public status retains the branch
The public status retains PR #5
The obsolete no-PR residue is removed

evals.prompt: Reach the audit-evidence validator with a parseable envelope whose report block disagrees with the returned audit evidence.
evals.expected_output: The run reaches AUDIT_EVIDENCE_UNVERIFIED with a concrete report-block mismatch path while preserving branch and PR residues.
expectations: Bug 6 is reachable
The error path and rule are concrete
Branch and PR residues remain visible

evals.prompt: Inspect the SOL start and resume prompts and prove that one machine-readable result-envelope schema supplies all 18 top-level fields plus every nested key and type.
evals.expected_output: Both prompts contain the generated exact contract and canonical example, while runtime structural validation consumes the same frozen schema before semantic normalization.
expectations: The schema contains exactly 18 top-level fields
Nested keys and primitive types come from structured child definitions
Start and resume render the same generated contract and example

evals.prompt: Validate canonical complete implementation and audit result envelopes against the host contract.
evals.expected_output: Both canonical envelopes validate and produce phase ready_for_quality_gate without relaxing delivery, audit, PR, or SOL-to-Terra requirements.
expectations: The implementation envelope reaches ready_for_quality_gate
The audit envelope reaches ready_for_quality_gate
All strict delivery and audit evidence checks remain active

evals.prompt: Return tests_and_verification as an object instead of its documented array and inspect the public error.
evals.expected_output: The host fails closed with CODEX_RESULT_ENVELOPE_INVALID and reports path, rule, and the sanitized expected array type without echoing the received value.
expectations: The result is blocked before the quality gate
validation_error identifies tests_and_verification:type_mismatch
validation_error includes the sanitized expected array type and no raw value

evals.prompt: Inject unknown top-level and nested secret fields into a job report before MCP formatting and inspect both text and structuredContent.
evals.expected_output: The strict public projection drops all unknown data, sanitizes expected, preserves valid public residues and result data, and never leaks the injected strings.
expectations: Unknown top-level and nested fields are absent from structuredContent
Raw expected text is replaced by an allowlisted public description
Valid branch, PR, recovery, and result evidence remains available

evals.prompt: Replay the real 1CDB3BEA, F1275700, and 8DA59548 runs plus every terminal reason without Codex, GitHub, or network access.
evals.expected_output: Every public terminal response preserves the strongest host-observed branch, commit, and PR residue; the successful 8DA reference remains ready for the quality gate.
expectations: All required terminal states are produced from fixtures in one offline command
Observed branch or PR evidence can never terminate with empty leftover_resources
The three real-run regression fixtures produce their expected public outcomes

evals.prompt: Validate a future audit envelope with audit_evidence:null against the exact-head committed report and exact audited-SHA source ranges.
evals.expected_output: The host derives one authoritative audit-evidence object from the verified report, hydrates the public result, and returns a bounded field-level mismatch for a conflicting legacy envelope.
expectations: Future audit envelopes contain no second hand-authored evidence copy
Host hydration requires an exact-head full report and byte-exact cited ranges
Legacy mismatches expose bounded redacted envelope-versus-artifact previews

evals.prompt: Inspect the new pull-request path after the paired 1CDB3BEA and F1275700 approval outcomes.
evals.expected_output: Terra remains read-only, SOL performs mutations and final proof reads in the observable parent thread, and PR creation accepts only the exact canonical marker as the entire body.
expectations: The denial is classified as an active external approval-policy rejection rather than a timeout
SOL owns every GitHub mutation and final proof read in the main thread
A new PR body is exactly the canonical marker with no other bytes

evals.prompt: Inspect a completed implementation pull request and prove that its human-readable explanation is preserved without changing the marker-only pull-request body.
evals.expected_output: The body remains the exact canonical marker and one bounded, neutral, host-verified same-PR comment records Problem, Change, Rationale, and Verification including checked scope.
expectations: The pull-request body remains exactly the canonical marker
One same-PR comment preserves Problem, Change, Rationale, and Verification with checked scope
The comment is bounded and contains no mention, URL, or gate sentinel; audit tasks create no context comment

evals.prompt: Exercise denial, timeout, abort, retry, and resume behavior around the implementation context-comment write.
evals.expected_output: Any failed first comment attempt preserves the already-created branch and pull request, a second attempt is rejected before mutation, and a resumed run cannot duplicate or alter verified comment evidence.
expectations: DENIED, TIMEOUT, and ABORTED comment writes preserve the branch and pull request
A second add_comment_to_issue attempt is rejected before it can succeed
A resumed run reuses persisted proof and cannot duplicate, edit, replace, or re-fetch the comment

evals.prompt: Audit every Codex Automatic-review observation and local fixture reference for evidence date, epistemic status, and guarantee language.
evals.expected_output: Historical behavior is dated and labeled observed or reported rather than guaranteed, the current local reference fixture is named explicitly, and the context comment's effect on an already-started Automatic review remains live-unverified.
expectations: Historical automatic-review observations are dated and not presented as guarantees
final-issue-comment-template-pr4.json is named as the current local reference
The neutral comment's external Automatic-review effect is explicitly live-unverified

evals.prompt: Inspect a completed implementation pull request and prove that its explanation survives without any GitHub write beyond the already necessary commit and pull request.
evals.expected_output: The PR body remains byte-exactly marker-only while the final branch-effective commit carries one bounded run-bound message with Problem, Change, Rationale, and Verification.
expectations: The final pull-request body is byte-exactly the original canonical marker-only line
The final branch-effective implementation commit preserves Problem, Change, Rationale, and Verification with checked scope
The explanation adds no comment write or comment fetch and audit behavior remains unchanged

evals.prompt: Exercise direct-file and low-level commit binding, multiple commits, no-op resume, and a real correction without adding an approval-gated context write.
evals.expected_output: The host accepts only the latest valid branch-effective explained commit, validates low-level update_ref using branch_name, persists an exact private resume seed, and requires a fresh message for every real correction.
expectations: Opaque successful update_ref results are accepted while optional contradictory rich fields fail closed
A no-op resume may reuse only an exact validated private seed
A later real correction replaces the seed with a fresh explained branch-effective commit and the final PR head must match it

evals.prompt: Audit the Automatic-review evidence hierarchy after removing the implementation context-comment write.
evals.expected_output: Historical review behavior stays dated and non-guaranteed, the current local fixture remains explicit, and implementation explanations use necessary commit messages rather than a separate review-affecting comment.
expectations: Historical automatic-review observations remain dated and are not presented as guarantees
final-issue-comment-template-pr4.json remains the named current local reference
The implementation explanation is carried by an already necessary commit message, not a separate PR comment
